# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Release Workflow
on:
  push:
    tags:
      - '*'

jobs:
  build-and-release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout Code
      uses: actions/checkout@v2
      
    # Assumes the presence of a package.json for Node.js applications or a setup.py file for Python ones
    - name: Set up Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v1
      with:
        node-version: 14
    
    - name: Install Dependencies (Node.js)
      run: npm ci
      
    # The actual build command may vary, this is just a placeholder. Replace accordingly.
    - name: Build Application
      run: npm run build
      
    - name: Release to GitHub
      uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
      with:
        token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        tag_name: ${{ github.ref }} # Uses the tag that was pushed as part of the event trigger.
      
    - name: Publish to npm/Yarn
      run: npm publish
      
    - name: Release to PyPI
      uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1
      with:
        user__token: ${{ secrets.PYPI_API_TOKEN }} # You will need to set this in your GitHub repository settings
